steps.title: Spot the phenomenon that needs explaining
steps.content:
The passage opens by highlighting that members of the silversword alliance show an extraordinary range of phenotypes—everything from vines to trees.

This is the central phenomenon the passage addresses, and the rest explains how it arose: a single ancestral tarweed diversified into distinct species as its descendants adapted to different habitats.

steps.title: Match the purpose to the choices
steps.content:
Choice C states the passage's exact purpose: describing the plant family and explaining how it became so varied.

Choices A and B expand scope beyond the alliance (to all Hawaiian plants)—A also mischaracterizes multiple possible ancestors—while D focuses on one species' habitat instead of the group-wide diversification.

That matches choice C—C is correct.
